questions.question: What was Vahan Teryan's real name?
questions.explanation: Vahan Teryan's real name was Vahan Sukiasi Ter-Grigoryan.

questions.question: What was the title of Vahan Teryan's first collection of works and when was it published?
questions.explanation: His first collection, "Մթնշաղի անուրջներ" (Twilight Dreams), was published in 1908.

questions.question: In what year did Vahan Teryan found the "Pantheon" publishing house?
questions.explanation: Vahan Teryan founded the "Pantheon" publishing house in 1912.

questions.question: Which of the following works did Vahan Teryan translate from Armenian to Russian?
questions.explanation: He translated Raffi's "Sparks" (first volume), Sundukyan's "Pepo", and Shirvanzade's "Evil Spirit" from Armenian to Russian.

questions.question: Which of Vahan Teryan's patriotic poem series was published in the "Mshak" newspaper in 1915?
questions.explanation: The patriotic poem series "Երկիր Նաիրի" (Land of Nairi) was published in the "Mshak" newspaper in 1915.

questions.question: What severe illness did Vahan Teryan suffer from, and where did he ultimately pass away?
questions.explanation: Vahan Teryan showed signs of tuberculosis in 1916 and died in Orenburg in 1920.

questions.question: From the early days of Soviet power, who was Vahan Teryan a close associate of?
questions.explanation: From the first days of Soviet power, Vahan Teryan became a close associate of Stalin.

questions.question: Vahan Teryan participated in the signing of which significant peace treaty, under a mandate signed by Lenin?
questions.explanation: He participated in the signing of the Brest Peace Treaty with Lenin's mandate.

questions.question: Vahan Teryan participated in the creation of "Poetry of Armenia" and "Collection of Armenian Literature" alongside which two prominent figures?
questions.explanation: Vahan Teryan participated in the creation of these books alongside Valeri Bryusov and Maxim Gorky.

questions.question: When did Vahan Teryan pass away?
questions.explanation: Vahan Teryan passed away on January 7, 1920, in Orenburg.
